title: Claude is a Reasoning Engine
voiceScript: Stop treating Claude like Google, and you'll ship wrong work faster. The instinct is to ask a question and trust the answer. I let it confirm a metric from memory, and it was confidently wrong. Claude isn't a search engine, it's a reasoning engine. It shines when I feed it real context, a messy SOP, a broken codebase, then ask for a decision. The engine is strong. It holds a million tokens of context, scores about 87 percent on coding benchmarks, and reaches you through six doors, one brain. Claude is a powerful assistant, not an oracle. It rewards context plus verification. Stop calling it a chatbot. Follow for more.

scenes.headline: An assistant, not an *oracle*

scenes.voiceText: Stop calling it a chatbot. Follow for more.
scenes.cta: What should it do for your business?

source: LinkedIn post + guide section 'What Claude Actually Is (and Isn't)'. Core thesis: Claude is a reasoning engine, not a search engine or chatbot; it rewards context + verification, not blind trust. Failure mode: let it confirm a metric definition from memory, sounded confident but was wrong, dashboard logic drifted a week. Right way: feed exact inputs (messy SOP, long client thread, half-broken codebase), ask for assumptions, force citations, verify names/numbers/sources before client workflows. One engine, many doors: chat (drafting/thinking), coding tools (shipping fixes), tool-using/agent mode (acting inside guardrails). Real numbers from source: holds up to 1M tokens of context (~several thick books); ~87% on a standard real-world engineering benchmark (SWE-bench); 2026 long-form factuality test = hallucinates far less than a leading competitor; 6 surfaces/'doors' (chat web, desktop app, phone app, coding tool, autonomous desktop agent, developer interface). Limits: knowledge cutoff (not a live index), confident hallucination, non-deterministic answers.
